product_updates.title: OpenAI’s Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it’s shutting down
product_updates.content: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

product_updates.title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
product_updates.content: A "major artificial intelligence company" reportedly offered a Kentucky family $26 million to build a data center on their farm.

product_updates.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
product_updates.content: Anthropic’s new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.

product_updates.title: Spotify tests new tool to stop AI slop from being attributed to real artists
product_updates.content: The idea behind the new tool is to give artists more control over which tracks are associated with their name on Spotify.

product_updates.title: Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
product_updates.content: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.

product_updates.title: Arm is releasing the first in-house chip in its 35-year history
product_updates.content: Arm is producing its own CPU for the first time. It developed the CPU with Meta, which is also the chip's first customer.

product_updates.title: OpenAI’s plans to make ChatGPT more like Amazon aren’t going so well
product_updates.content: OpenAI says it's moving away from Instant Checkout, which allowed users to buy items directly through the ChatGPT interface.

product_updates.title: Google TV’s new Gemini features keep fans updated on sports teams and more
product_updates.content: Three Gemini-powered features are coming to your Google TV. This includes visual responses, deep dives, and sports briefs.

product_updates.title: OpenAI adds open source tools to help developers build for teen safety
product_updates.content: Rather than working from scratch to figure out how to make AI safer for teens, developers can use these policies to fortify what they build.

product_updates.title: Talat’s AI meeting notes stay on your machine, not in the cloud
product_updates.content: The subscription-free AI meeting notes app is a local-first twist on notetaking tools like Granola.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Exclusive eBook: Are we ready to hand AI agents the keys?
technical_breakthroughs.content: We’re starting to give AI agents real autonomy, but are we prepared for what could happen next? This subscriber-only eBook explores this and angles from experts, such as “If we continue on the current path … we are basically playing Russian roulette with humanity.” by Grace Huckins June 12, 2025 …

technical_breakthroughs.title: This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain
technical_breakthroughs.content: L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits cushioned in a vat at a storage facility in Arizona. It has been held there at a temperature of around −146 degrees °C for over a decade, largely undisturbed. That is, apart from the time, a little over a year ago, when scientists slowly lifted the brain to take photos…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
technical_breakthroughs.content: Development houses: It's time to check your networks for infections.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: tracing AI-fueled delusions, and OpenAI admits Microsoft risks
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions What actually happens when people spiral into delusion with AI? To find out, Stanford researchers anal…
